title: Deepen the codebase
question: Where should the architecture deepen next?
lede: Candidate seam directions at equal fidelity; analysis only.
inputs: the modules and tests the analysis is grounded in

currentMap.caption: Today: catalog.mjs owns both the data and the filtering; app.mjs owns both wiring and empty-state construction; tests import across two seams.

options.label: Split data from filtering
options.claim: catalog.mjs quietly grew two jobs — the skill data and the filtering logic. Splitting them makes the data swappable without touching behavior.
options.flowCaption: skills-data.mjs becomes a pure data module; filter.mjs depends on nothing but its input shape.
pros: a real win observed in the code
options.blueprintSeed: Scope / non-goals / criteria this direction would hand to change-blueprint.

options.label: Give the empty state a home
options.claim: app.mjs builds the accepted empty state inline — markup, chips, and copy in glue code. Extracting empty-state.mjs makes the accepted design a named, tested artifact.
options.flowCaption: empty-state.mjs renders from the same data island the catalog uses; app.mjs shrinks to pure wiring.
pros: a real win observed in the code
options.blueprintSeed: Scope / non-goals / criteria this direction would hand to change-blueprint.

options.label: Fold url-tag into catalog
options.claim: Two modules for forty lines may be one seam too many — folding parseTagFromSearch into catalog.mjs trades a boundary for simplicity.
options.flowCaption: One module owns tag semantics end to end; app.mjs imports a single surface.
pros: a real win observed in the code
options.blueprintSeed: Scope / non-goals / criteria this direction would hand to change-blueprint.

recommendation.reason: It is the only direction that turns an accepted decision (the empty state) into tested, named code — the other two move boxes without changing what can fail. The seed is small, the criteria are mechanical, and it reduces the file most likely to grow badly.

unresolved: conditions that would reopen the rejected directions
